title: lut2mux: add -word option
body:
lut2mux by default emits gate-level $_MUX_. In some flows it’s nicer to keep muxes as generic $mux cells (word-level) instead of immediately lowering to $_ gate cells. This adds an opt-in for that; default output stays the same.

Add an optional -word flag in lut2mux.cc. When set, the pass uses addMux() instead of addMuxGate() at the mux instantiation points during the $lut decomposition. The decomposition itself is unchanged and only the mux cell type differs with -word on.

Testing:
make -C tests/techmap techmap-lut2mux.ys (or run the test via tests/techmap/run-test.mk)
The test constructs a simple $lut in RTLIL, runs lut2mux and lut2mux -word on separate module copies (gate / word), asserts expected cell types per module, then proves they're equivalent with equiv_*.

You can also run: 
yosys -p "read_rtlil <lut_netlist.il>; lut2mux; stat"
yosys -p "read_rtlil <lut_netlist.il>; lut2mux -word; stat"

In the first case you should see $_MUX_ cells; in the second you should see $mux cells.
